title: typeof
subtitle: What's inside?
goal: Learn how to check the type of any value in JavaScript.
explanation: typeof is an operator that tells you the type of a value.
It helps you understand whether a value is a number, text, boolean, or another type.
This is very useful when debugging programs and checking data.
typeof always returns a result as a string.
It is one of the simplest tools for exploring values in JavaScript.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: typeof is like a magnifying glass or scanner that looks inside a box and reports what is inside.

remember: typeof determines the type of a value.
The result of typeof is always a string.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think typeof returns the type itself. In fact, it returns the type name as text, such as "number" or "string".
